tagline: Let kids roam. Stay in range.
elevator_pitch: Roamy is a lightweight GPS wristband for kids that replaces bulky smartwatches with simple geofence technology. Parents set a safe zone, kids get freedom to explore, and everyone stays worry-free.
problem: Modern parents want to give their kids independence but live in a culture of helicopter parenting. Existing GPS watches are expensive, require monthly cellular plans, and ironically reinforce constant surveillance rather than fostering freedom.
solution: Roamy is a slim, wristband-style GPS tracker that uses geofencing instead of two-way calling. Parents draw a safe zone on a map, and the band gently alerts the child when they approach the boundary. No monthly cell plan, no distracting features, just peace of mind and real freedom.
target_market: Parents of children ages 5 to 13 who want to encourage outdoor play and independence while maintaining a basic safety net. Especially relevant for suburban families and parents who grew up with more freedom themselves.
revenue_model: Direct-to-consumer hardware sales at $49 to $79 per band, with an optional premium app tier ($3/month) for advanced features like relay network tracking, multi-child dashboards, and historical route data. No mandatory monthly subscription required for core functionality.
key_features: Customizable geofence zones drawn directly on a parent's phone map
Slim, Livestrong-style wristband design that kids actually want to wear
Tile-style relay network so nearby Roamy parents extend the tracking mesh
No monthly cellular plan required for core GPS and alert functionality
competitive_advantage: Unlike chunky kids' smartwatches that cost $200 plus $15/month in cell service, Roamy is affordable, minimal, and philosophically different. It promotes independence instead of surveillance, which resonates with parents who remember the freedom of their own childhoods.
market_size_estimate: The global kids GPS tracker market is valued at roughly $1.5 billion and growing at 15% CAGR. With 50 million US households with children under 13, even 1% penetration at $60 average selling price represents $30 million in revenue.
go_to_market: Launch on Kickstarter or Indiegogo to validate demand and build a community of early-adopter parents. Partner with parenting influencers and mommy bloggers for organic reach. Sell direct-to-consumer via web store, then expand into Target and Amazon.
risks: Parents may prefer the two-way calling features of existing smartwatches over a simpler device
Large tech companies like Apple or Google could add geofencing features to their existing kids' products
Privacy concerns around GPS tracking children could create regulatory or PR challenges
year_one_milestones: Launch crowdfunding campaign and sell 5,000 units in first 90 days
Ship version 1.0 hardware and companion app for iOS and Android
Establish relay network with 10,000 active Roamy users in 3 metro areas
